content:
Incident Report
Page 5 of 11
Detective Sergeant Kevin M. Engelbertson, #2D1 Lewis County Sheriff's Office Chehalis, WA
KME : kvw September 10, 2020
SUPPLEMENTAL NARRATIVE: Name: Brown T Date: 10:10:54 09/09/20 SUPPLEMENTAL/T Brown/9-9-20
AGENCY CASE NUMBERS : Lewis County Sheriff's Office 20C11252 Grays Harbor County Sheriff's Office 20-16695 Thurston County Sheriff's Office 20-4433 Washington State Patrol 20-014825
SUPPLEMENTAL :
On 9/3/20 at approximately 2010 hours, I, Detective Tyson Brown, was contacted by phone by Detective Sergeant Engelbertson that the Region 3 Critical Incident Investigation Team (CIIT) had been activated regarding an officer involved shooting in the Lacey, WA area. Detective Sergeant Engelbertson later provided an incident address of 7601 3rd Way SE, Lacey. I was requested to initially respond to the Lacey Police Department at 420 College St SE, Lacey where the involved officers and witness officers were awaiting contact.
Detective Sergeant Engelbertson and I arrived at Lacey PD at approximately 2104 hours. Detective Scott Robb arrived shortly after our arrival. Shortly upon our arrival, we received brief incident details from Thurston County Sheriff's Office Detective Hamilton. Detective Hamilton had informed us that members of the US Marshall Task Force had tracked a homicide suspect from Portland, OR to an apartment complex at 7601 3rd Way SE, Lacey. Detective Hamilton said he received information that the suspect, who was later identified as Michael Reinoehl, was seen putting a rifle case into a silver colored Honda. He was then seen getting into a beige colored Volkswagen as law enforcement attempted contact with the suspect. Upon making contact, the suspect was believed to be armed and was disobeying commands. Detective Hamilton also said law enforcement began shooting into the vehicle. The suspect then attempted to flee on foot as the shooting continued. The suspect then went down approximately 30-40 feet into the foot pursuit.
I was requested to process one of the involved officers. I was partnered with Thurston County Detective Sean Chatterton. It should be noted that other detectives who are not a part of the Region 3 CIIT were present to assist due to the need for additional resources. One of the Region 3 CIIT detectives were assigned to a non-member.
Detective Chatterton and I were later introduced to Craig Gocha from the Pierce County Sheriff's Department. Gocha had informed us that he is employed by Pierce County, and he is also a full-time task force operator for the US Marshall's
rplwdir.x1
09/14/20
